data.title: Pakistan Just Passed a Crypto Law Covering 40 Million Users and $20 Billion in Digital Assets
data.description:
Pakistan’s National Assembly passed the Virtual Assets Act 2026 on March 5, ending years of legal ambiguity for one of the world’s largest informal crypto markets. The legislation creates a dedicated regulatory authority, mandates licensing for all crypto service providers, and introduces a capital gains tax on digital asset conversions.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/pakistan-just-passed-a-crypto-law-covering-40-million-users-and-20-billion-in-digital-assets/

data.title: Software Stocks Achieve Historic Outperformance Against Semiconductor Sector
data.description:
The Kobeissi Letter posted on X. Software stocks have recently experienced a significant surge, with the Tech-Software ETF, $IGV, outperforming the Semiconductor Sector ETF, $SOXX, by 16.59 percentage points over the past six trading sessions. This marks the largest six-day outperformance on record. During this period, $IGV has rallied by 8.37%, reaching its highest level since February 2nd, while $SOXX has declined by 8.22%, nearing its lowest point since February 5th.

This development represents a notable reversal from the end of January when software stocks underperformed by nearly 15 percentage points, the largest gap since 2008. The recent surge in software stocks coincides with an increase in hedge fund short exposure to U.S. software and services stocks, which reached a record 3.8% last week. As a result, a short squeeze in software stocks has occurred, contributing to their historic outperformance against the semiconductor sector.

data.title: U.S. President Trump to Honor Fallen Soldiers at Dover Air Force Base
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced plans to visit Dover Air Force Base with the First Lady and cabinet members to pay respects to fallen soldiers. According to Jin10, this visit underscores the administration's commitment to honoring military service and sacrifice. The President's itinerary includes a solemn ceremony to acknowledge the contributions of the nation's military personnel.

data.title: Top Global Asset Management Firms' Bitcoin Holdings Revealed
data.description:
Bitcoin treasury company Strategy has disclosed the top ten holdings of global asset management firms on the X platform. According to PANews, the list is led by The Vanguard Group, which holds an 8.12% stake valued at $3.183 billion. Capital Research & Management follows with a 7.7% stake worth $3.019 billion. BlackRock Fund Advisors holds a 3.64% stake valued at $1.428 billion, while another holding by Capital Research & Management is at 2.62%, valued at $1.026 billion.

SSGA Funds Management has a 2.29% stake worth $897 million, and Morgan Stanley holds a 2.08% stake valued at $815 million. UBS Securities owns a 2.02% stake worth $793 million. Amundi Asset Management SASU holds a 1.77% stake valued at $693 million. Geode Capital Management has a 1.46% stake worth $573 million, and Norges Bank Investment Management rounds out the list with a 1.32% stake valued at $517 million.

data.title: Whale Address Opens Leveraged ETH Short Position
data.description: On March 7, a whale address identified as '0x218A' deposited 2.18 million USDC into HyperLiquid, according to BlockBeats. The address then opened a 10x leveraged short position on ETH. Previously, this whale had profited over $11.8 million through trading activities.

data.title: US Private Credit Market Faces Decline Amid AI Concerns
data.description:
The Kobeissi Letter posted on X. The U.S. private credit market is experiencing a downturn, with the median listed Business Development Company (BDC) now trading at just 0.73 times its net asset value (NAV), marking the lowest point since 2020. BDCs, which are publicly traded entities lending to small, mid-sized, and distressed U.S. businesses, provide retail investors access to private credit markets. Currently, they are valued at 73% of their claimed worth.

Over the past 18 months, the median listed BDC price-to-NAV ratio has been steadily declining. For context, this ratio plummeted to approximately 0.35 times during the 2008 financial crisis, indicating that the market valued BDCs at merely 35 cents on the dollar.

This decline occurs amid growing concerns that artificial intelligence (AI) could disrupt the business models of software firms, complicating efforts to refinance debt. The average BDC holds around 20% exposure to the software sector, which is under scrutiny due to potential AI impacts.

The private credit industry is facing significant pressure as these challenges unfold, raising questions about the future stability and valuation of BDCs in the market.

data.title: U.S. Advances in Controlling Iranian Airspace, Says White House
data.description: The White House announced progress in controlling Iranian airspace, with expectations to achieve objectives within four to six weeks. According to Jin10, White House Press Secretary Levitt stated this during a press briefing on March 6. Earlier, U.S. President Donald Trump declared on social media that the U.S. would not reach any agreement with Iran except for 'unconditional surrender.' Levitt clarified that 'unconditional surrender' implies that once the U.S. deems Iran no longer a threat and military objectives are met, Iran will effectively be in a state of 'unconditional surrender,' regardless of any public declaration from Iran.

data.title: Arkham: An address associated with Justin Sun holds over $880 million in sUSDS, potentially yielding annual returns of $35 million.
data.description: Arkham posted on the X platform that an address associated with Justin Sun recently deposited $250 million into sUSDS, bringing its total holdings of sUSDS tokens to over $880 million. It is estimated that this address can earn approximately $35 million in returns annually.

data.title: PancakeSwap responds to plagiarism allegations: Will contact the Curve team directly to discuss the matter.
data.description: In response to Curve Finance's accusation that PancakeSwap appears to have used Stableswap code and borrowed security practices to protect user funds without permission, violating its open-source license agreement, PancakeSwap stated that it will contact the Curve Finance team directly to discuss the matter.

data.title: BlockFills Seeks Restructuring Amid Financial Challenges
data.description:
BlockFills, a cryptocurrency trading and lending firm, is reportedly seeking restructuring advice from consulting firm BRG. According to ChainCatcher, the company has appointed Mark Renzi from BRG as the 'Chief Transformation Officer' following a halt in withdrawals. The new management aims to complete the restructuring process, secure new capital, and implement improved governance and financial controls.

Last month, BlockFills temporarily suspended customer deposits and withdrawals, attributing the decision to 'recent market and financial conditions' following a significant downturn in the digital asset market. This week, a federal judge issued a temporary restraining order against BlockFills after a client, Dominion Capital, accused the company of mismanaging client funds in February.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Proposes Minimmit to Replace Casper FFG in Ethereum Consensus
data.description:
Vitalik Buterin has suggested replacing the current finality component, Casper FFG, with a new mechanism called Minimmit in Ethereum's consensus discussions. According to Odaily, Buterin explained that Casper FFG uses a two-round finality process, requiring validators to sign twice for 'proof' and 'final confirmation.' In contrast, Minimmit achieves finality with just one round of signatures. As a trade-off, Minimmit's fault tolerance threshold would decrease from Casper FFG's 33% to approximately 17%.

Despite the reduced fault tolerance, Minimmit is more likely to lead the network into a 'multi-chain competition' rather than an erroneous final confirmation in various attack or client bug scenarios. This situation is easier to recover from through community coordination. Buterin believes that avoiding finality for a short period is safer and more recoverable than having incorrect blocks finalized, suggesting that Minimmit may offer a better overall security balance.

data.title: BlackRock Adjusts Ethereum Staking ETF Fee Structure
data.description: BlackRock has updated its Ethereum staking ETF application, reducing the staking fee rate to 10% of staking rewards, according to PANews. Bloomberg analyst James Seyffart shared on the X platform that the latest document indicates potential tiered rate discounts based on scale. Previously, the ETF's staking fee was set at 18% of total staking earnings. This fee reduction is seen as part of an optimization of BlackRock's Ethereum product structure.

data.title: White House Unveils Cyber Strategy Highlighting Cryptocurrency and Blockchain
data.description:
The White House has released a new cyber strategy document titled 'President Trump's Cyber Strategy for America,' according to PANews. The seven-page document outlines six main pillars, focusing on cyber offense and deterrence, but lacks detailed implementation plans. Notably, it is the first U.S. cybersecurity strategy to explicitly mention cryptocurrency and blockchain technology, emphasizing the need for their protection and security. This contrasts with the national cybersecurity strategy released by Joe Biden in 2023, which did not address these technologies.

The strategy also aims to 'eradicate criminal infrastructure and cut off financial exit channels,' supported by a new executive order targeting transnational cybercrime. Analysts suggest this could provide regulatory agencies with a basis to intensify actions against mixers, privacy coins, and unregulated financial channels.

Additionally, the strategy proposes expanding offensive cyber operation authorities and plans to involve the private sector in combating hostile networks. It also advocates for AI-driven automated cyber defense systems. A new operational unit will be established within the National Coordination Center to coordinate law enforcement against transnational cybercrime groups.

data.title: Arthur Hayes reiterated that HYPE could reach $150, with escalating tensions in the Middle East driving on-chain oil trading.
data.description: BitMEX founder Arthur Hayes stated in an article on the X platform that market price discovery may shift to crypto-native platforms during the weekend when traditional exchanges are closed. He pointed out that against the backdrop of escalating conflicts in the Middle East, Hyperliquid's oil perpetual contract CL-USDC may become an important price discovery market for oil price movements over the weekend. Hayes further reiterated that with increased trading activity, the target price for Hyperliquid's platform token HYPE could reach $150.

data.title: New Restaurant at Hyatt Centric Elevates Peking Duck Competition in Hong Kong
data.description: A new restaurant at the Hyatt Centric in Hong Kong is raising the stakes in the city's competitive Peking duck scene. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the restaurant's elegant approach to this traditional dish. Known for its high standards, Hong Kong's culinary landscape is witnessing increased competition among establishments specializing in Peking duck. The new venue at Hyatt Centric aims to distinguish itself with a refined dining experience, contributing to the vibrant and diverse food culture in the city.

data.title: Former Twitter Executives Challenge Elon Musk's Claims in Court
data.description: Two former senior executives from Twitter are contesting Elon Musk's allegations in a jury trial. Bloomberg posted on X that the executives are accused of misleading Musk about the composition of Twitter's user base during his acquisition of the company in 2022. The trial seeks to address Musk's claims that he was deceived about the platform's user metrics, which he argues influenced his decision to purchase Twitter. The outcome of this trial could have significant implications for both parties involved.

data.title: As Trump confronts Anthropic, the US drafts strict new guidelines for civilian AI.
data.description: As the Trump administration confronts Anthropic, it has drawn up strict rules for civilian AI contracts, requiring AI companies to allow "any legal use" of their models. A draft of new government guidelines stipulates that AI companies wishing to do business with the government must grant the U.S. an irrevocable license to use their systems for all legal purposes. This guidance from the General Services Administration will apply to civilian contracts and is part of a broader government effort to strengthen the procurement of AI services. A source familiar with the matter said the guidelines are similar in principle to measures the Department of Defense is considering for military contracts. (Financial Times)

data.title: Tehran's Mehrabad Airport Reportedly Hit by Intense Airstrike
data.description: Tehran's Mehrabad Airport has reportedly been subjected to a severe airstrike, according to Iranian media. The attack on the airport, located in Iran's capital, has raised concerns about escalating tensions in the region. Details about the extent of the damage or potential casualties have not been disclosed. The incident underscores the fragile security situation in the area, prompting international attention and calls for restraint from involved parties.

data.title: OpenAI releases Codex Security, an AI agent security tool.
data.description: OpenAI has announced the launch of an AI-powered agent designed to help security teams discover and fix vulnerabilities in large databases, a move that could erode demand for traditional cybersecurity firms. In a statement Friday, OpenAI said the agent, named Codex Security, can identify cybersecurity vulnerabilities and propose solutions before fixing the errors. The company says the tool is designed to “run at scale” and deliver “accessible patches,” allowing developers to focus on higher-level tasks. The company also revealed that the agent has been used to scan and identify security vulnerabilities in open-source code repositories. This product competes with Anthropic PBC’s Claude Code Security, which can also discover vulnerabilities and suggest remediations. Last month, the release of Claude’s new security tool caused a drop in cybersecurity stocks. At that time, Crowdstrike and Cloudflar both fell by 8%.

data.title: ParaFi Capital pledged 70 million SKY tokens that it withdrew yesterday, four hours ago.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Yu Jin, ParaFi Capital pledged 70 million SKY tokens that were withdrawn yesterday, worth approximately $5 million, four hours ago.
